mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power with t-stat below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicate strong consumer demand supported by accommodative policy, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, with interaction term coefficients statistically insignificant at p greater than 0.10 in a multivariate regression over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding corporate capacity utilization and earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month specifically during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration predicts negative or flat returns during expansion phases, or the 20-day forward return correlation drops below 0.05 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 260.0, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL breaches 260.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized labor and housing market strength, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal yields no incremental predictive power over single indicators, with interaction effects statistically insignificant at p greater than 0.15 over 4 years.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter specifically during late-cycle downturns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines predict positive returns during late-cycle phases, or the 60-day forward return correlation remains above 0.05 in rolling 3-year windows.
